concern_reasons.note: truncated flag can be set when no clamping occurred; test asserts reimplemented predicate
AC3 tests field shape only, not rendered stdout column format
real handle_query/run_serve path has no integration test
tests/mocks/ac6.rs is dead (not wired by mocks.rs); should be deleted

counter_attack.description: A test calling handle_query with limit=100/cap=50 and a stub recall returning only 3 hits would expose the truncated=true-when-nothing-clamped bug.
counter_attack.test_skeleton: #[test] fn would_break_truncated_when_few_hits() { unimplemented!("stub: recall returns 3 hits, limit=100, cap=50, assert truncated==false") }

falsification.deps_audit: 11 deps (<=max_deps 12). All mainstream crates. futures-util added for StreamExt. No advisory crates.
falsification.drift_audit: iter-1 diff is API-compat/clippy/test fixes only. No scope drift from PRD.
falsification.panic_audit: No unwrap/expect/panic in src/ on the happy path. No panics on the public happy path.
falsification.public_api_audit: limit=usize::MAX is safe (clamped by min). Malformed recall JSON returns Err (not panic). No UB reachable.
falsification.test_audit: AC2 snippet truncation and AC4 read-only invariant are well-covered. AC1/AC5/AC7 tests are honest and verify the stated behaviors. AC2 truncated-flag and AC3 output-format tests are shallow.
falsification.unsafe_audit: No unsafe present. deny_unsafe: true honored throughout via #![deny(unsafe_code)].
